ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general oolong tea beverage class; no specific Four Seasons cultivar entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers oolong tea infusion as a category; no cultivar-specific code for Four Seasons (Si Ji Chun) exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 refers to oolong tea as a substance class; Four Seasons cultivar is not individually coded.

main_ingredients: Four Seasons oolong tea leaves (Camellia sinensis var. Si Ji Chun)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one cup) of brewed Four Seasons Oolong Tea, which weighs approximately 250 g given the near-water density of the beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, oolong, brewed (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 14366)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea used as the closest approved source. Four Seasons (Si Ji Chun) is a specific Taiwanese oolong cultivar; its brewed nutrient profile is essentially identical to generic brewed oolong tea at the per-100 ml level. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central for brewed oolong tea (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, negligible macronutrients). Served as a plain brewed beverage without added sugar or milk at Happy Cup - Funan.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Beverages — Plain brewed tea (oolong); no glycemic carbohydrates present
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed oolong tea contains negligible carbohydrates (approximately 0.1 g per 100 ml) and no glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database does not list a specific GI value for plain oolong tea because it contains insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response. By convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value), gi_category is set to low, and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. This is consistent with the treatment of plain black tea and green tea in the GI literature.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content; potential mild vasodilatory effect from tea polyphenols
hypertension.guidance: Plain Four Seasons Oolong Tea contains approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml and no added salt, making it a suitable beverage for individuals managing hypertension; regular oolong tea consumption has been associated with modest reductions in blood pressure in observational studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and zero glycemic load when consumed without added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain Four Seasons Oolong Tea contains approximately 0.1 g carbohydrate per 100 ml and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it a safe beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; polyphenols in oolong tea may also support insulin sensitivity, though added sugar must be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of antioxidant tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain Four Seasons Oolong Tea is free of saturated fat and cholesterol and contains antioxidant catechins and theaflavins that may support endothelial function; it is a heart-friendly beverage when consumed without added sugar or full-fat milk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Four Seasons Oolong Tea (四季乌龙茶, Sì Jì Wūlóng Chá) is a brewed tea beverage made from the Si Ji Chun (Four Seasons Spring) cultivar of Camellia sinensis, a semi-oxidised oolong variety originally cultivated in Taiwan and also grown in Fujian, China. The name 'Four Seasons' refers to the cultivar's ability to be harvested multiple times per year across all four seasons. The tea is prepared by steeping dried and partially oxidised tea leaves in hot water, producing a pale golden to amber liquor with a floral, lightly fruity aroma and a smooth, mildly astringent taste with a characteristic lingering sweetness. It occupies a flavour profile between green tea and fully oxidised black tea. Nutritionally, plain brewed Four Seasons Oolong Tea is essentially calorie-free, containing approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml, negligible protein, zero fat, and only trace carbohydrates (approximately 0.1 g per 100 ml). It contains no glycemic carbohydrates and has a glycemic load of zero per serving, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The beverage is rich in polyphenolic antioxidants including catechins, theaflavins, and thearubigins, and contains caffeine at moderate levels (approximately 30–50 mg per 250 ml serving). In Singapore, Four Seasons Oolong Tea is commonly served at bubble tea shops and Chinese tea houses, both hot and iced, and may be offered plain or with added sugar, milk, or toppings. The record at Happy Cup - Funan reflects a plain or lightly sweetened brewed version priced at SGD 7.40.
common_user_queries: What is Four Seasons Oolong Tea?

Does Four Seasons Oolong Tea have caffeine?

Is Four Seasons Oolong Tea vegan?
Is oolong tea good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for generic brewed oolong tea, which is the closest approved source. The Four Seasons (Si Ji Chun) cultivar is a specific Taiwanese oolong variety; its brewed nutrient profile is not individually listed in any primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). The USDA entry for brewed oolong tea is a defensible and well-established proxy. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact cultivar-specific entry was found in an approved primary source.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 ml, reflecting the near-zero caloric content of plain brewed oolong tea per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 2. protein_g is naturally zero — plain brewed tea contains no measurable protein. 3. fat_g is naturally zero — plain brewed tea contains no fat by nature. 4. carbohydrate_g is 0.1 g per 100 ml, representing trace soluble compounds leached from tea leaves; confirmed per USDA SR Legacy. 5. sugar_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for unsweetened brewed oolong tea). 6. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — fibre does not leach into brewed tea liquor). 7. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. 8. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 9. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 10. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 12. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 13. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed oolong tea. 16. The restaurant record (Happy Cup - Funan, field survey data) may serve this tea with added sugar or toppings; the nutrient values here reflect plain unsweetened brewed Four Seasons Oolong Tea. 17. Caffeine content is approximately 30–50 mg per 250 ml serving but is not captured in the per_100g schema as caffeine is not a standard field. 18. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no cultivar-specific codes exist for Four Seasons oolong tea in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT.
